proteingym_assays.csv.note: Per-assay wildtype sequence and metadata (12 ProteinGym DMS assays).

proteingym_dms.csv.gz.note: Variant-level ProteinGym fitness with additive prediction, measured epistasis, and 7 frozen splits (values train/test/val/unused). Reconstruct mutant sequence from proteingym_assays.wildtype_sequence + mutations.

tsuboyama_stability_doubles.csv.note: Tsuboyama double mutants: measured ddG (score), additive prediction, measured epistasis (raw, |.|, per-domain z), and Cbeta-Cbeta distance (A) between the two mutated residues.

tsuboyama_all_variants.csv.gz.note: All Tsuboyama variants (singles + doubles): measured ddG and full mutant sequence.

skempi_binding.csv.note: SKEMPI multi-mutant binding: order k (2-8), measured ddG of binding, additive prediction, and measured epistasis.

skempi_epistasis_type.csv.note: SKEMPI double-mutant epistasis type from signed component ddGs: additive/magnitude/sign/reciprocal-sign (threshold 0.3 kcal/mol). Repeated (pdb,muts) rows are independent measurements.

maad_escape.csv.gz.note: Antibody x SARS-CoV-2 variant neutralization (label: 1=escape/non-neutralized). Antibody identity + VH/VL/CDRH3 sequence, viral variant + mutation metadata, and 9 frozen splits (train/test).

proteingym_generalization_leaderboard.cs.note: Reference scores: 31 models x 12 assays x 7 splits -> Spearman/Pearson/RMSE/MAE (generalization task).

tsuboyama_epistasis_leaderboard.csv.note: Reference scores (matches eval/evaluate.py T2): per-domain |Spearman| between predicted and directly-measured double-mutant epistasis on the unsaturated subset (score & additive_pred within [15,85]% per domain), >=15 doubles/domain, median over domains. Additive models score ~0 by construction.

tsuboyama_epistasis_predictions_per_doub.note: Per-double reference: measured epistasis (+|.|, z) and each model's predicted epistasis (8 models, incl. ESM-C and PoET), with Cb distance.

maad_escape_leaderboard.csv.note: Reference scores: additive vs interaction-capturing classifier AUROC/AUPRC per MAAD split.
